h2h_summary: The Netherlands and Morocco have faced each other three times in all-time head-to-head matches, with the Netherlands winning twice and Morocco winning once. All three historical encounters ended with a 1-2 scoreline.
team_style_home: The Netherlands under Koeman play a possession-based 4-2-3-1 that morphs into a fluid 4-3-3 in attack, combining aggressive man-oriented pressing with quick vertical transitions and overlapping full-backs.
team_style_away: Morocco deploy a compact 4-1-4-1 or 4-3-3, excelling in disciplined defensive organisation and rapid transitions, with Achraf Hakimi driving forward on the right and Brahim Diaz creating between the lines.
wc_history_home: The Netherlands have been World Cup runners-up three times (1974, 1978, 2010) and are one of the most storied nations in tournament history, despite never lifting the trophy.
wc_history_away: Morocco made history at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar by becoming the first African and Arab nation to reach the semi-finals, finishing fourth after stunning wins over Spain and Portugal.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: World-class defensive core led by Virgil van Dijk, one of the best defenders in the world
Prolific attack with Memphis Depay (all-time Dutch top scorer) and Cody Gakpo providing cutting edge
Deep squad with Premier League and Champions League experience across multiple positions
strengths_away: World-class individual talent led by Achraf Hakimi and Brahim Diaz
Elite defensive organisation and transition play built over years under Regragui and now Ouahbi
Strong tournament pedigree and mental resilience from their 2022 semi-final run
